title: About the Commodity Data Collection Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Commodity Data Collection Survey
21 September 2023, 10:45am
Information about taking part in the Commodity Data Collection Survey, such as why we carry out the survey, how we selected you, and how to complete the questionnaire.
We carry out this survey to collect information to:
- balance the production and expenditure estimates of gross domestic product (GDP) within an input-output framework
- determine the supply and use of commodities by industries
- update lower-level weights for the business price indexes
- use as deflations in producing a chain-volume measure of GDP.
The target population is all New Zealand businesses. We select approximately 3,500 business units to participate throughout the eight-year redevelopment cycle.
A selection of businesses in each industry will be surveyed once in that time. A business reporting unit is assigned a single industry, and most industries will only be surveyed once in the eight-year development cycle.
Your business may be selected if the industry your business is identified with is selected to participate during the eight-year redevelopment cycle period. You will be asked about the detailed breakdown of the types of goods and services that businesses buy and sell.
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Commodity Data Collection Survey.
This is a rolling data collection. We will be surveying businesses once every eight years.

## How to complete the survey
You fill in a paper questionnaire.
- Gross domestic product
- Contact us for survey help 26 May 2020
- Business survey information 31 July 2017
- Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data 20 May 2026
- Business 01 January 2017 Image: work table meeting
